name: gcsfs (GCS Filesystem Interface for Python)
pricing_notes: Open source (BSD-3); fsspec ecosystem; GCS API costs apply

auth.notes: Application Default Credentials (gcloud auth), service account JSON, or token

performance.notes: GCS API calls per operation; caching supported; async for parallel operations

agent_readiness.notes: GCS filesystem interface — GCP equivalent of s3fs. import gcsfs. fs = gcsfs.GCSFileSystem(project='my-project'). with fs.open('my-bucket/data.parquet', 'rb') as f: df = pd.read_parquet(f). pd.read_parquet('gs://my-bucket/data.parquet') uses gcsfs automatically. Dask, Zarr, xarray integration. fs.ls(), fs.glob(), fs.rm(). Async: gcsfs.GCSFileSystem(asynchronous=True). Token scopes configurable. Part of fsspec — same API as s3fs/adlfs for cross-cloud portability. Handles GCS resumable uploads for large files.

gotchas: Credential setup more complex than s3fs (gcloud auth vs AWS credential chain)
ls() on deep paths generates many API calls
Token refresh requires application re-auth in long-running processes
GCS object versioning not exposed through filesystem interface
best_when: Reading/writing Parquet, CSV to GCS with pandas/dask/xarray
GCP data pipelines wanting filesystem semantics on GCS
Code that switches between local and GCS paths transparently
BigQuery ML data pipelines reading from gs:// URIs
avoid_when: GCS-specific features (lifecycle, ACLs, signed URLs — use google-cloud-storage)
summary: gcsfs provides filesystem-like access to Google Cloud Storage. fs = gcsfs.GCSFileSystem(project='my-project'). fs.open(), fs.ls(), fs.glob(), fs.rm(). pandas/dask/xarray use gcsfs transparently via pd.read_parquet('gs://bucket/file'). Part of fsspec — same API as s3fs and adlfs for cross-cloud portability. Async support. For high-performance transfers use google-cloud-storage SDK directly. GCP equivalent of s3fs.
